q.en: What disease did Jonas Salk develop a vaccine for?
q.zh: 乔纳斯·索尔克研发了针对哪种疾病的疫苗？

options.en: Influenza according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 流感（按书中另一种说法）
小儿麻痹症（脊髓灰质炎）

explain.en: Dr. Salk told the nation 'a vaccine for polio was on the way.'
explain.zh: 索尔克博士告诉全国“小儿麻痹症疫苗即将问世”。

q.en: How many types (strains) of polio did Salk's team determine exist?
q.zh: 索尔克的团队确定脊髓灰质炎有几种（型）？

explain.en: 'Salk's team determined that there were only three types of polio' in one year.
explain.zh: 索尔克的团队在一年内“确定脊髓灰质炎只有三种类型”。

q.en: What kind of vaccine did Salk believe would work and be safer?
q.zh: 索尔克认为哪种疫苗既有效又更安全？

explain.en: 'Salk believed that a killed-virus vaccine... would work just as well—plus, it was safer.'
explain.zh: “索尔克相信灭活病毒疫苗……同样有效——而且更安全。”

q.en: On what date was it announced that the polio vaccine worked?
q.zh: 是在哪一天宣布小儿麻痹症疫苗有效的？

options.en: 1952 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: 'On April 12, 1955... Francis announced... The vaccine works.'
explain.zh: “1955年4月12日……弗朗西斯宣布……疫苗有效。”

q.en: The book includes a 'How Antibodies Work' diagram and a chart 'Polio in the United States through the Years.' These features —
q.zh: 书中包含“抗体如何工作”的图解和“历年美国小儿麻痹症”的图表。这些设置——

options.en: Are decorative only according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 只是装饰（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Help explain science with visuals and data
options.zh: 用视觉和数据帮助解释科学

explain.en: Diagrams and charts make the science clearer and give evidence over time.
explain.zh: 图解和图表让科学更易理解，并提供了随时间变化的数据证据。

q.en: The book uses a Focus Question ('How did Jonas Salk achieve his goal of making a difference for humanity?'). This question —
q.zh: 本书使用了一个焦点问题（“乔纳斯·索尔克如何实现造福人类的目标？”）。这个问题——

options.en: Sets a purpose for reading and guides thinking
options.zh: 为阅读设定目的并引导思考

options.en: Gives the ending away according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 透露了结局（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: A focus question gives readers a goal and frames what to look for while reading.
explain.zh: 焦点问题为读者设定目标，并框定阅读时要寻找的内容。

q.en: In the field trial, some children received the vaccine, some a placebo, and the rest were a control group. What is a placebo?
q.zh: 在实地试验中，一些孩子接种了疫苗，一些用了安慰剂，其余作为对照组。什么是安慰剂？

options.en: The real vaccine according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 真正的疫苗（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A substance with no physical effect, used for comparison
options.zh: 没有生理作用、用作对比的物质

explain.en: The glossary defines a placebo as 'a pill or substance that has no physical effect on a patient, often used as a control.'
explain.zh: 词汇表把安慰剂定义为“对病人没有生理作用的药丸或物质，常作为对照使用”。

q.en: What went wrong after the vaccine was distributed in 1955?
q.zh: 1955年疫苗分发后出了什么问题？

options.en: One manufacturer had not properly killed the virus, causing some polio cases
options.zh: 一家厂商没有正确灭活病毒，导致一些小儿麻痹症病例

options.en: The vaccine was a total failure according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 疫苗完全失败（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: 'Investigators soon tracked the problem to one manufacturer that had not properly killed the virus.'
explain.zh: “调查人员很快把问题追到一家没有正确灭活病毒的厂商。”

q.en: What is the author's main purpose?
q.zh: 作者的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To inform readers about how Salk developed the polio vaccine and helped humanity
options.zh: 向读者介绍索尔克如何研发小儿麻痹症疫苗并造福人类

options.en: To sell sugar cubes according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了卖糖块（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The biography explains Salk's scientific work and its huge benefit to people.
explain.zh: 这本传记解释了索尔克的科研工作及其对人类的巨大益处。

q.en: How does the author view Salk's achievement?
q.zh: 作者如何看待索尔克的成就？

options.en: As a great difference for humanity; he achieved his goal
options.zh: 造福人类的一大贡献；他实现了目标

options.en: As unimportant according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 无关紧要（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book ends: 'Salk achieved his goal of making a difference for humanity.'
explain.zh: 书的结尾写道：“索尔克实现了造福人类的目标。”

prompt.en: Write a short paragraph explaining how a vaccine teaches the body to fight a disease, using the idea of antibodies from the book.
prompt.zh: 用书中抗体的概念，写一小段话解释疫苗如何让身体学会对抗疾病。

en: Say why a killed-virus vaccine is safe.
zh: 说明为什么灭活病毒疫苗是安全的。
把它和索尔克的工作联系起来。
